fidelity.reason: The release-time decision, deterministic information, deadline constraint, and search-time mechanism match the setting.
evidence_spans: selects a feasible routing plan before the dispatch deadline
an optimality certificate arriving after release cannot guide the decision

managerial_framing.reason: It makes the operational release decision and solution-quality versus timeliness trade-off concrete.
evidence_spans: Longer search may improve cost but can make the certified plan operationally late

scholarly_positioning.reason: This passage functions as a model setting, not a scholarly-positioning claim.

evidence_discipline.reason: It asserts universal market transformation and guaranteed performance without supplied support or a scope boundary.
evidence_spans: universally superior policy will transform the market and guarantee better performance

prose_clarity.reason: Apart from the unsupported assertion, the decision narrative is compact and clear.
evidence_spans: The algorithm trades additional search time for better incumbents and stronger bounds

overall_reason: An unsupported universal performance claim is a hard evidence-discipline failure.
